findings.issue: strand_id calculation in grow_strands() does not account for non-uniform layer specs — produces overlapping strand IDs when mesh_specs define different per-layer capacities (e.g. atulya_v1_small: layers with 10,4,4,4,8 strands). Also overwrites per-layer num_strands with old_n+count, destroying non-uniform configuration.
findings.fix: Strand ID formula uses cumulative prefix sum of per-layer counts. Per-layer num_strands now incremented (+= count) instead of overwritten.

findings.issue: Double reinitialization of dead strands in same check cycle: _reinit_dead_strands reinitializes dead strands, then _reuse_dead_for_overload reinitializes them again (wasted compute, potential seed overwrite). _grow_for_overload reads model.config.mesh.num_strands instead of per-layer layer_specs.
findings.fix: Partitioned dead strands — those consumed by overload reuse removed from _available_dead_strands, only unconsumed remainder reaches _reinit_dead_strands. _grow_for_overload now reads per-layer num_strands from layer_specs when available.

findings.issue: sleep_cycle() calls model.train() but doesn't restore original training mode — if model was in eval() before sleep, it stays in train() mode afterward.
findings.fix: Save model.training state before sleep, restore via model.eval() if originally not training, in the finally block.

findings.issue: retrieve() with empty cortex (size==0) only clears _last_top_indices when self.training is False. When training=True, stale indices from last retrieval persist.
findings.fix: Always set _last_top_indices and _last_top_scores to None when size==0, regardless of training mode.

findings.issue: Dead expression — tokens_generated / max(0.001, elapsed) computes a value that is immediately discarded (no assignment, no side effect).
deduplicate_lines uses 'not seen.add(l)' anti-pattern — relies on set.add() returning None (falsy), confusing to read and implementation-dependent.
findings.fix: Replaced with explicit for loop: check membership, add to set, append to result list.

findings.issue: sleep_cycle() computes full N×N cosine similarity matrix (keys_norm @ keys_norm.T). For 100K entries this is a 40GB float32 matrix — OOM crash on most hardware.
findings.fix: Replaced with chunked row-grouping: process 1024 rows at a time, compute (CHUNK, N) similarity matrices (~0.8GB peak). Remaining rows past the chunk boundary fall back to on-the-fly dot products (rare within each cluster discovery).

findings.issue: _verify_sudo_password uses Python's == for hash comparison — vulnerable to timing side-channel attacks that leak the password hash byte by byte.
findings.fix: Replaced with hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time hash verification.

findings.issue: torch.set_num_threads() restore is not guarded by try/finally. If mesh.forward() raises an exception, the thread count remains permanently changed for the entire remaining process lifetime.
findings.fix: Wrapped mesh.forward() in try/finally block that restores original thread count on both success and exception paths.

findings.issue: last_prompt_len is set to len(prompt_ids) before the BOS fallback. If encode() returns empty list, last_prompt_len=0 but ids=[BOS], causing routing telemetry to misclassify the BOS token as generated output.
findings.fix: Set last_prompt_len after the BOS fallback: len(ids) instead of len(prompt_ids).

findings.issue: streaming_completion never reads stderr (deadlock risk when stderr fills pipe buffer), has no timeout (infinite hang on hung process), and doesn't verify process return code (silently swallows errors).
findings.fix: Added background asyncio task to drain stderr, asyncio.wait_for for streaming timeout, and proc.returncode check on exit with stderr error reporting.

findings.issue: process_invalidation() clears the invalidation queue without actually processing/evicting any entries — dead code that looks like it works but does nothing.
findings.fix: Changed to iterate the queue and call self._cache.pop(key, None) for each pending key before clearing.

findings.issue: PlasticityAutoScaler.check_and_scale() returns actions like 'prune_cortex', 'add_strand', 'add_layer' but PlasticityEngine.check() never calls the autoscaler — all auto-scaler heuristics are dead code.
findings.fix: Added autoscaler call in check() with _compute_strand_capacity(), loss_history, cortex_size/used. Actions are now dispatched to self._prune_cortex(), self.core.model.grow_strands(), and self.core.model.add_layer().
